fields.description:
## Context

DSO planning workflows silently bypass scrutiny in two ways: (1) inferred decisions affecting high-blast-radius fields (gate_verdicts, workflow_completion_checklist) ship without adversarial challenge; (2) discovered-too-late questions defer past the point of user attention. The 3dae-af1a self-attestation incident (CLI_user-tagged, open) is the canonical failure mode: a brainstorm orchestrator emitted a comment claiming a scrutiny rationale had been logged, when in fact no red-team-reviewer dispatch had occurred — there was no structural artifact (ticket comment, signal, attestation field) that a downstream check could fail on. The bypass shape is "agent asserts a procedural outcome the agent itself was responsible for producing." This epic delivers the structural channel that makes such bypasses visible at the inference-decision layer.

This is one half of a two-epic split. Sibling epic (degradation channel) covers PRECONDITIONS degradation:bool field, sprint story-closure forcing function, and acknowledgment dual-channel. Split was made on scrutiny review (2026-04-26) because the original combined epic spanned two independently shippable user goals.

## Glossary

- PRECONDITIONS: event log defined by epic 736d-b957 (closed). JSONL append-only artifact under the ticket-tracker tree, written by ticket-lib.sh._write_preconditions, read via preconditions-validator-lib.sh.
- decisions_log: array within a PRECONDITIONS event payload recording decisions made during a planning step (rationale, source, affects_fields).
- 818-corpus: historical fixture of 100 closed bugs at tests/fixtures/818-corpus/sample-bugs.json. Spike 2 (2026-04-27) confirmed this corpus does NOT contain inference-decision provenance — it describes implementation bugs. SC4 reformulated to use a separately-curated inference-incident corpus instead.
- META_QUESTION: NEW signal type defined by SC1 of this epic; emitted when a brainstorm question's blocking_for resolves to brainstorm itself.
- REPLAN_ESCALATE: existing signal used for mid-workflow question discovery routing back to upstream phase.
- session_id: stable identifier from the harness session-id file (verified existing; Spike 1, 2026-04-27).

## Approach

Foundation-then-features. Story 1 (foundation) delivers four contracts: decision_id format (plugins/dso/docs/contracts/decision-id-format.md), inference-envelope (plugins/dso/docs/contracts/inference-envelope.md), META_QUESTION signal contract, and inference-incident-schema (plugins/dso/docs/contracts/inference-incident-schema.md) plus the dso:inference-incident-curator agent definition (plugins/dso/agents/inference-incident-curator.md, opus tier) and its first-run corpus output. Stories 2-4 (parallel) deliver brainstorm completeness attestation, dso:red-team-reviewer inference-challenge mode, and the inference-recall replay measurement. Story 5 runs the dogfooding validation.

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 12 (all accepted; analysis covered combined epic before 2026-04-26 split — both halves share the scenario evidence)
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: scenario analysis run once; fidelity review run three times across combined → split-A → split-B revisions
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: triggered — depth: 5 spikes (A-Verification-1 session_id existence; A-Spike-1 818-corpus inference-recoverability; B-Spike-1 SC2 marker mechanism; B-Spike-2 SC4 harvest-sync sequencing; B-Spike-3 SC3 keyword detector FP baseline)
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: triggered — cycles: 3, gap: SC6c semantic CI detector mechanically infeasible (resolved via downgrade to keyword-with-suppression on diff-scoped pre-commit pattern, then further simplified by Spike 5 to staged-diff scanning); also resolved unverified SC2 marker mechanism and SC4 harvest-sync sequencing concerns via spikes that revealed structural simplifications
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: triggered — keyword: skill file modifications (epic modifies brainstorm Phase 1 Gate prose and dso:red-team-reviewer agent prompt; sibling epic modifies 11 skill files)
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: not applicable (no volume decision)

## Success Criteria

SC1: Brainstorm completeness attestation embedded INSIDE the existing Phase 1 Gate (a sub-step of the gate's exit condition, not a separate gate that follows it). Phase 1 Gate cannot return passed without an attestation field of value exhausted or open. Open lists each unresolved question with blocking_for: <phase>. If blocking_for resolves to brainstorm itself (meta-question), escalate to user via explicit META_QUESTION signal — do NOT REPLAN_ESCALATE (which would loop). Mid-workflow question discovery from later phases continues to route via REPLAN_ESCALATE.

SC2: dso:red-team-reviewer gains an inference-challenge prompt mode. Every invocation MUST emit a typed envelope, never silence: either INFERENCE_CHALLENGE with findings, or INFERENCE_SKIP{reason: sampling | not_in_scope, hash_bucket: N}. Silence is treated as a dispatch bug — orchestrator files a tracking ticket and logs a warning, must NOT apply fail-open. Tiered sampling: 100% review when affects_fields includes gate_verdicts or workflow_completion_checklist; 20% deterministic (hash(decision_id) mod 5 == 0) for rationale-text-only inferences. The affects_fields classifier MUST be tested for misclassification rate against a hand-labeled fixture stored at tests/fixtures/affects-fields-classifier.json containing >=50 high-stakes and >=50 rationale-only labeled examples drawn from the most recent 6 months of planning sessions; required accuracy >=95% on high-stakes class.

SC3: Inference challenges surface as ticket comments with structured payload {decision_id, challenge_type, evidence_against_inference, user_confirmation_required: bool}. decision_id format: <session_id>:<content_hash> — session-scoped (cross-worktree resumes match) and content-stable (re-emission produces same id). session_id sourced from the harness session-id file with fallback to DSO_SESSION_ID env or generated pv-session-<timestamp_ms>. content_hash is SHA-256 of normalized decision text, truncated to 12 chars for readability.

SC4: Inference-recall validation against an LLM-curated set of historical planning-inference incidents. Curator deliverable: dso:inference-incident-curator at plugins/dso/agents/inference-incident-curator.md (opus tier — distinguishing genuine inference-mediated failures from tickets merely containing trigger words is a subtle classification task with high blast radius; sonnet would over-include false positives). When dispatched the curator: (a) scans closed tickets via the ticket-list CLI filtered by keyword patterns ("inferred", "assumed", "rationale", "wrong inference", "should have asked", "fell back to default") plus tickets carrying inference-bug tags; (b) for each candidate ticket extracts inferred decision text (verbatim from comment or description), affects_fields (gate_verdicts | workflow_completion_checklist | rationale_text), eventual outcome that proved the inference wrong, and ticket reference; (c) emits structured JSONL at tests/fixtures/inference-incidents/incidents.jsonl conforming to plugins/dso/docs/contracts/inference-incident-schema.md; (d) anti-hallucination validation: each incident MUST reference a real ticket-id (verified inline by the ticket-exists CLI), the inferred-decision text MUST be a fuzzy 3-word-window substring match against the live ticket text, and a 5-incident deterministic random sample (hash(incident_id) mod (N/5)) requires human review attestation via paired comment on the inference-incident-schema contract doc by a non-curator author. Curator targets >=20 incidents on first run; re-runnable quarterly. Held-out split deterministic via hash(ticket_id) mod 3 == 0 selecting ~33% into tests/fixtures/inference-incidents/holdout.txt; remainder forms tuning set. Pass criterion (in-session closure gate, not post-deploy): replay shows challenges would have surfaced >=50% of all incidents AND >=70% of held-out partition specifically; precision (fired challenges mapping to real incidents / total fired) >=0.5. If first run produces fewer than 10 incidents, SC4 escalates with CORPUS_INSUFFICIENT signal.

SC5: Sampling rates tunable via preconditions.inference_review.rate_rationale and preconditions.inference_review.rate_high_stakes in dso-config.conf. Absent keys default to 20 and 100 respectively, with a one-time install-time log entry stating the active default. The config validator enforces a minimum floor of 100 on rate_high_stakes — values below 100 are rejected at config-load time with a non-zero exit, refusing to start the affected skill. rate_rationale accepts 0-100.

SC6: During a dogfooding sprint covering >=3 brainstorm sessions, >=1 user-visible META_QUESTION prompt is surfaced that the user answers with a non-default response. Demonstrates the forcing function produces real course-corrections, not rubber-stamps. Captured via PRECONDITIONS events filtered on signal_type=META_QUESTION with user_answer != null.

## Dependencies

Depends on (structural prerequisite, documented but not link-tracked due to closed-target restriction): 736d-b957 (PRECONDITIONS schema/writer infrastructure — closed); existing dso:red-team-reviewer.

Blocks: sibling degradation-channel epic (provides decision_id format and INFERENCE_SKIP envelope shape that the sibling reuses).

Relates to: 6068-cb2d (completion verifier — coordination on attestation schema); 6111-fc7f (structural sentinel — Phase 1 Gate brainstorm coordination); 4b8f-924d (severity-only review schema — SC2 envelope conforms if it lands first); 1d4f-9feb (sprint drift detection — consumes SC1 attestation open entries); ca48-c3c3 (closed; SC1 extended its brainstorm context completeness work); d63f-2c74 (operational atomicity check — shares brainstorm exit gate surface); 5df7-63ac (observability — may consume PRECONDITIONS events for telemetry); 853a-c719 (preplanning observability — same); dso-8l5h (extract review/eval sub-agents — inference-incident-curator fits this pattern); 736d-b957 (foundation, closed).

## Out of Scope

PRECONDITIONS schema/writer infrastructure (already shipped in 736d-b957). The C2 sprint validator + degradation acknowledgment work (lives in sibling degradation-channel epic). Graceful-degradation prose instrumentation (sibling). Truths/artifacts/key_links verifier work (lives in 6068-cb2d). The 818-corpus bug-coverage harness (Spike 2 confirmed schema mismatch; remains in scope of broader manifest program, not this epic).
